preamble: Retroflexion of the lateral [l] to [ɭ]. This distinction is preserved in several Indo-Aryan languages like Punjabi, Marathi, and Gujarati, often distinguishing between historical dental and retroflex/geminate laterals.

examples.note: Phonemic distinction between dental and retroflex laterals.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
